title: AI Agent Guide: Modular WalletVerificationScene Architecture
description: Comprehensive guide for AI agents working with the refactored WalletVerificationScene to prevent issues and maintain code quality.

overview.purpose: The WalletVerificationScene has been completely refactored from a monolithic 1200+ line file into a modular architecture with separate managers for different concerns.
benefits: Better type safety with TypeScript interfaces
overview.architecture: The new architecture follows the Manager pattern with dedicated classes for different aspects of wallet verification

mainScene.description: Main scene orchestrator that coordinates all managers

types.description: TypeScript interfaces and types for type safety

mobileHandler.description: Handles mobile device detection and mobile-specific UI
keyFeatures: Multi-method mobile detection (device OS, user agent, screen size)
commonIssues: Mobile detection false positives - use multiple detection methods
Touch feedback not working - ensure proper event listener setup
Resize handling - call handleResize() on screen orientation changes

walletUIManager.description: Manages all UI components and visual effects
keyFeatures: Message display system (success, error, warning, info)
commonIssues: Button not responding - check if setInteractive() is called
Message timers not cleaning up - use activeMessageTimers array
Gradient not updating - call drawGradient() after resize

walletConnectionManager.description: Handles wallet connection logic and blockchain interactions
keyFeatures: Existing wallet checks and confirmation dialogs
commonIssues: Web3Service not initialized - ensure Web3Service is passed to constructor
Player data validation failing - check Firestore document existence
Wallet binding conflicts - handle existing wallet confirmation properly

nftDisplayManager.description: Manages NFT grid display, scrolling, and interactions
commonIssues: Images not loading - check CORS and image URL validity
Scrolling not working - ensure scroll zone is properly set up
Grid layout broken - validate gridConfig calculations
Memory leaks - always call cleanup() to remove event listeners
Content container not initialized - ensure createMainContainer() creates both nftContainer and contentContainer
currentScrollConfig null reference - pass scrollConfig as parameter to createNFTGrid instead of relying on uninitialized currentScrollConfig
Z-order conflicts - use appropriate depth values following project hierarchy (NFT display should use depth 1800 for modal overlay)

managerInitialization.description: All managers follow a consistent initialization pattern
pattern: 1. Constructor receives scene and required dependencies
2. Initialize() method sets up internal state
4. Cleanup() method removes listeners and destroys objects

errorHandling.description: Standardized error handling across all managers
pattern: 1. Use try-catch blocks for async operations
2. Return structured result objects with success/error states

rules: Always remove event listeners in cleanup()
Store timer references for proper cleanup
Handle resize events in all UI managers

internal.name: Database utilities
internal.note: Not directly imported in main scene, used by WalletConnectionManager

errors.error: Property 'getChainId' does not exist on type 'Web3Service'
errors.solution: Use getNetwork() method instead and parse chainId from result
errors.fix: const network = await this.web3Service.getNetwork(); const chainIdNumber = parseInt(network.chainId, 16);
errors.prevention: Always check Web3Service implementation for available methods before using

errors.error: 'ProgressState' is declared but its value is never read
errors.solution: Remove unused import from types import statement
errors.prevention: Regular cleanup of unused imports during development

errors.error: Property 'activeMessageTimers' does not exist on type 'WalletUIManager'
errors.solution: Add missing property declaration in class
errors.prevention: Initialize all referenced properties in constructor or class declaration

errors.fix: interface SimpleButtonConfig { text: string; fontSize: string; backgroundColor: string; color: string; padding: { x: number; y: number }; cursor?: boolean; }
errors.prevention: Define all required types and interfaces before using them

errors.solution: Make createModernButton method public as required by interface
errors.prevention: Ensure class method visibility matches interface requirements

errors.error: 'MESSAGE_STYLES' is declared but its value is never read
errors.fix: Delete the entire MESSAGE_STYLES constant object
errors.prevention: Remove properties immediately when they become unused during refactoring

errors.error: 'isMobile' is declared but its value is never read
errors.solution: Remove unused parameter from method signature
errors.prevention: Clean up method parameters when refactoring

errors.error: 'createStyledButton' is declared but its value is never read
errors.fix: Delete the createStyledButton and updateButtonBackground methods
errors.prevention: Remove methods immediately when they become unused during refactoring

compilationErrors.error: Property 'X' does not exist on type 'Y'
compilationErrors.solution: Check interface definitions in types.ts and ensure all required properties are defined
compilationErrors.prevention: Always update interfaces when adding new properties to managers

compilationErrors.error: Type 'Promise<X>' is not assignable to type 'X'
compilationErrors.solution: Check interface method signatures match implementation (async vs sync)
compilationErrors.prevention: Ensure interface definitions match implementation signatures exactly

compilationErrors.solution: Verify file paths and ensure all modular components are in the correct directory
compilationErrors.prevention: Use relative paths consistently and check file structure

runtimeErrors.solution: Ensure initializeManagers() is called before using any manager methods
runtimeErrors.prevention: Follow the scene lifecycle: initialize → validate → setup → create UI

runtimeErrors.solution: Ensure createMainContainer() creates both nftContainer and contentContainer, add contentContainer as child
runtimeErrors.prevention: Always initialize all required containers in createMainContainer() method

runtimeErrors.solution: Pass scrollConfig as parameter to createNFTGrid instead of accessing uninitialized currentScrollConfig
runtimeErrors.prevention: Initialize configuration objects before use or pass as parameters

runtimeErrors.error: Z-order conflicts or UI elements not appearing correctly
runtimeErrors.solution: Follow project depth hierarchy: NFT display should use depth 1800 for modal overlay above all game elements
runtimeErrors.prevention: Reference established depth standards: Map(0-4), Player(8), NPCs(10), UI(999-1000), Special UI(1000-2000)

runtimeErrors.solution: Call cleanup() on all managers in shutdown() method
runtimeErrors.prevention: Always implement cleanup() method in all managers

runtimeErrors.solution: Implement handleResize() in all UI managers and call on scale.on('resize')
runtimeErrors.prevention: Test on different screen sizes during development

development: Always implement interfaces for managers to ensure consistency
Use TypeScript strict mode and fix all type errors
Follow the single responsibility principle for each manager
Implement proper error handling with structured return objects
Use dependency injection for better testability
testing: Test each manager independently with mock dependencies
Verify mobile and desktop functionality separately
Test error scenarios and edge cases
Test wallet connection flows with different wallet states
maintenance: Keep interfaces updated when adding new functionality
Document any breaking changes in this guide
Use semantic versioning for major architectural changes
Maintain consistent code style across all managers
Regular code reviews focusing on manager interactions

steps: 1. Determine which manager should handle the new feature
2. Update the relevant interface in types.ts
3. Implement the feature in the appropriate manager
4. Update the main scene if coordination is needed
5. Test the feature in isolation and integration
6. Update this guide with any new patterns or issues
considerations: Does this feature fit the manager's responsibilities?
Does the UI need to be responsive?

strategy: 1. Identify which manager is responsible for the failing functionality
2. Check the manager's state and dependencies
4. Test manager in isolation with mock data
5. Check event listener setup and cleanup
6. Validate error handling and user feedback

memoryManagement: Always call cleanup() on scene shutdown
Remove event listeners to prevent memory leaks
optimization: Lazy load managers only when needed
Use object pooling for frequently created/destroyed objects

potentialImprovements: Add unit tests for each manager
Add analytics and user behavior tracking
Add configuration system for manager behavior
extensibility: Managers can be extended for additional wallet types
UI themes can be made configurable
Mobile detection can be enhanced with more device types
NFT display can support additional collection types

beforeModifying: ☐ Understand which manager handles the functionality

changes: Fixed TypeScript compilation errors across all files
Cleaned up unused imports and variables
Added missing property declarations and type definitions
fixedErrors: Property 'getChainId' does not exist on type 'Web3Service'
'ProgressState' is declared but its value is never read
Property 'activeMessageTimers' does not exist on type 'WalletUIManager'
'detectMobile' is declared but its value is never read

changes: Created 4 manager classes with clear responsibilities
Implemented TypeScript interfaces for type safety
breakingChanges: Complete refactor from monolithic to modular architecture
New file structure with wallet-verification directory
